Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

moving if (lnet_is_rdma_only_page(page->cp_vmpage)) into llite, save a lots time as it will one check per whole chunk not an one check per io chunk submission.

Patch Set 1:

Patrick, you've been testing the IO performance recently.  Can you please see what the performance impact of this patch is. 

Shadow's opinion is that it has a higher per-page impact, but his patches spread changes throughout the code and add complexity to the whole callpath.

Patch Set 1:

Sure!  I considered that before giving +1 here, actually; this patch checks just 1 page per RPC (which is all that’s required, since they’re all the same).

For smaller I/Os, that’s the same as doing it at the llite layer, and for larger I/Os, it’s still just 1/RPC.  If this were per page I would be concerned, but it’s just per RPC.  We do a *lot* of things per RPC, literally one more if won’t be measurable.

parents.subject: LU-14798 lnet: add LNet GPU Direct Support

commit.subject: LU-14798 lustre: Support RDMA only pages
commit.message:
LU-14798 lustre: Support RDMA only pages

Some memory architectures and CPU-offload cards with
on-board memory do not map data pages into the CPU
address space. Allow RDMA of data directly into those
pages without accessing contents.

Therefore, made changes to prevent doing checksum on
these type of pages.

Signed-off-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: Amir Shehata <ashehata@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I189c34893ffa500ed275f2a1f79e8fb817a2489d
lustre-change: https://review.whamcloud.com/37454
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
